generatedSentence: In an ancient mansion, under the ambient midnight glow, a young maiden toiled. She was a ghostwriter, her labour yielding little money, her once-bright luster dimmed. She felt illiterate in the ways of power, living a backward existence, longing to become more. Her only solace, a hidden alcove where she sipped applejack, dreaming of a life without this burden. A cruel tribunal from the formidable citadel ruled with an iron fist, led by a notorious cheater. Their advance guard, armored tanks and powerful cannon, applied tactical pressure. The maiden knew she needed a new technique and a powerful weapon to fight this waterlogged system. Her lament echoed in the vast ellipse of the city, a single bottle of desperation against a menacing wolf. With an enraged eagle cry, she ascended to an elevated platform, her interstellar inventory of makeshift items ready. She recalled the endless lessons, a mental keyboard of combat kits. Though jinxed by circumstance, she fought with the agility of a kangaroo, jostling through enemy lines. Her unique joint maneuvers led to strategic kills, leaving the enemy, like disorganized jellyfish, overwhelmed. The victory, though hard-won, marked a literal turning point. The corrupt system was botched, its bindings loosened. Her guerrilla tactics had secured a ticket to freedom, promising growth and significant gain. From the ashes, a new material future began, celebrated with a joyous jubilee. No longer a mere larva, she emerged an actress on a grand, infinite stage, even finding time to care for a stray kitty by a warm grill. The centrifuge of power had finally spun in her favor.
panelSentences: In an ancient mansion, under the ambient midnight glow, a young maiden toiled. She was a ghostwriter, her labour yielding little money, her once-bright luster dimmed. She felt illiterate in the ways of power, living a backward existence, longing to become more. Her only solace, a hidden alcove where she sipped applejack, dreaming of a life without this burden.

A cruel tribunal from the formidable citadel ruled with an iron fist, led by a notorious cheater. Their advance guard, armored tanks and powerful cannon, applied tactical pressure. The maiden knew she needed a new technique and a powerful weapon to fight this waterlogged system. Her lament echoed in the vast ellipse of the city, a single bottle of desperation against a menacing wolf.

With an enraged eagle cry, she ascended to an elevated platform, her interstellar inventory of makeshift items ready. She recalled the endless lessons, a mental keyboard of combat kits. Though jinxed by circumstance, she fought with the agility of a kangaroo, jostling through enemy lines. Her unique joint maneuvers led to strategic kills, leaving the enemy, like disorganized jellyfish, overwhelmed.

The victory, though hard-won, marked a literal turning point. The corrupt system was botched, its bindings loosened. Her guerrilla tactics had secured a ticket to freedom, promising growth and significant gain. From the ashes, a new material future began, celebrated with a joyous jubilee. No longer a mere larva, she emerged an actress on a grand, infinite stage, even finding time to care for a stray kitty by a warm grill. The centrifuge of power had finally spun in her favor.
rawPanelSentences: In an **ancient mansion**, under the **ambient midnight** glow, a young **maiden** toiled. She was a **ghostwriter**, her **labour** yielding little **money**, her once-bright **luster** dimmed. She felt **illiterate** in the ways of power, living a **backward** existence, longing to **become** more. Her only solace, a hidden **alcove** where she sipped **applejack**, dreaming of a life **without** this burden.

A **cruel tribunal** from the formidable **citadel** ruled with an iron fist, led by a notorious **cheater**. Their advance guard, armored **tanks** and powerful **cannon**, applied **tactical** pressure. The maiden knew she needed a new **technique** and a powerful **weapon** to fight this **waterlogged** system. Her **lament** echoed in the vast **ellipse** of the city, a single **bottle** of desperation against a menacing **wolf**.

With an **enraged eagle** cry, she ascended to an **elevated** platform, her **interstellar inventory** of makeshift **items** ready. She recalled the **endless** lessons, a mental **keyboard** of combat **kits**. Though **jinxed** by circumstance, she fought with the agility of a **kangaroo**, **jostling** through enemy lines. Her unique **joint** maneuvers led to strategic **kills**, leaving the enemy, like disorganized **jellyfish**, overwhelmed.

The victory, though hard-won, marked a **literal** turning point. The corrupt system was **botched**, its **bindings** loosened. Her **guerrilla** tactics had secured a **ticket** to freedom, promising **growth** and significant **gain**. From the ashes, a new **material** future began, celebrated with a joyous **jubilee**. No longer a mere **larva**, she emerged an **actress** on a grand, **infinite** stage, even finding time to care for a stray **kitty** by a warm **grill**. The **centrifuge** of power had finally spun in her favor.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, dynamic superhero action comic style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. The protagonist is a young woman wearing a shimmering dark-purple tech-suit and glowing emerald-green gauntlets. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The Maiden stands in an opulent yet dimly lit ancient mansion, gazing out a large window at the city's dark citadel, pensive, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The Maiden, hidden in an alcove, observes through high-tech binoculars as massive tanks and cannons deploy from the cruel citadel, medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The Maiden, agile like a kangaroo, engages enemy soldiers in futuristic combat on an elevated platform, glowing gauntlets striking, dynamic low-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The Maiden stands victorious amidst fallen tech, a subtle smile on her face, the sun rising over a hopeful city, looking towards new growth, triumphant full shot. Ensure cel-shading, bold ink outlines, and cinematic lighting, with NO text, NO speech bubbles, NO dialogue.

definitions.definition: A closed container for liquids or gases.
An open container or pool for storing water or other liquids.
A pond, pool, or small lake, natural or artificial.
The fuel reservoir of a vehicle.
The amount held by a container; a tankful.
definitions.example: I burned three tanks of gas on the drive to New York.

definitions.definition: An armoured fighting vehicle, armed with a gun, and moving on caterpillar tracks.
(Australian and Indian English) A reservoir or dam.
A large metal container for holding drinking water for animals, usually placed near a wind-driven water pump, in an animal pen or field.
By extension a small pond for the same purpose.
A very muscular and physically imposing person. Somebody who is built like a tank.
A unit or character designed primarily around damage absorption and holding the attention of the enemy (as opposed to dealing damage, healing, or other tasks).
A prison cell, or prison generally.
A metaphorical place where a player goes to contemplate a decision; see in the tank.
To fail or fall (often used in describing the economy or the stock market); to degenerate or decline rapidly; to plummet.
To attract the attacks of an enemy target in cooperative team-based combat, so that one's teammates can defeat the enemy in question more efficiently.
To put (fuel, etc.) into a tank.
To deliberately lose a sports match with the intent of gaining a perceived future competitive advantage.
To resist damage; to be attacked without being hurt.
To contemplate a decision for a long time; to go in the tank.
A small Indian dry measure, averaging 240 grains in weight.
A Bombay weight of 72 grains, for pearls.
To stand; to tolerate.
A combinator of proof tactics.
A private war reenactment event involving mock battles or skirmishes.
Of, or relating to tactics
Of, or relating to military operations that are smaller or more local than strategic ones
Adroit, skilful or ingenious
Having a military appearance, typically with accessories such as a bipod, adjustable stock, detachable magazine or black coloration
The practical aspects of a given art, occupation etc.; formal requirements.
Practical ability in some given field or practice, often as opposed to creativity or imaginative skill.
A method of achieving something or carrying something out, especially one requiring some skill or knowledge.
A pass entitling the holder to admission to a show, concert, etc.
A pass entitling the holder to board a train, a bus, a plane, or other means of transportation
A citation for a traffic violation.
A permit to operate a machine on a construction site.
A service request, used to track complaints or requests that an issue be handled. (Generally technical support related).
A list of candidates for an election, or a particular theme to a candidate's manifesto.
definitions.example: Joe has joined the party's ticket for the county elections.

definitions.definition: A solution to a problem; something that is needed.
definitions.example: I saw my first bike as my ticket to freedom.

definitions.definition: A little note or notice.
A tradesman's bill or account (hence the phrase on ticket and eventually on tick).
A label affixed to goods to show their price or description.
A certificate or token of a share in a lottery or other scheme for distributing money, goods, etc.
A visiting card.
To issue someone a ticket, as for travel or for a violation of a local or traffic law.
To mark with a ticket.
definitions.example: to ticket goods in a retail store

definitions.definition: An assembly including one or more judges to conduct judicial business; a court of law.
A kind of village hall used to transact business, to quarter troops and travellers, and to confine prisoners.
An increase in size, number, value, or strength.
definitions.example: Growth was dampened by a softening of the global economy in 2001, but picked up in the subsequent years due to strong growth in China.

definitions.definition: The act of growing, getting bigger or higher.
Something that grows or has grown.
An abnormal mass such as a tumor.
The act of gaining; acquisition.
What is gained.
The factor by which a signal is multiplied.
To acquire possession of.
definitions.example: Looks like you've gained a new friend.

definitions.definition: To have or receive advantage or profit; to acquire gain; to grow rich; to advance in interest, health, or happiness; to make progress.
definitions.example: The sick man gains daily.

definitions.definition: To come off winner or victor in; to be successful in; to obtain by competition.
definitions.example: to gain a battle; to gain a case at law

definitions.definition: To increase.
To be more likely to catch or overtake an individual.
definitions.example: I'm gaining (on you).

definitions.definition: To reach.
definitions.example: to gain the top of a mountain

definitions.definition: To draw into any interest or party; to win to one's side; to conciliate.
To put on weight.
definitions.example: I've been gaining.

definitions.definition: (of a clock or watch) To run fast.

A grating; a grid of wire or a sheet of material with a pattern of holes or slots, usually used to protect something while allowing the passage of air and liquids. Typical uses: to allow air through a fan while preventing fingers or objects from passing; to allow people to talk to somebody, while preventing attack.
On a vehicle, a slotted cover as above, to protect and hide the radiator, while admitting air to cool it.
A cooking device comprising a source of radiative heat and a means of holding food under it; a broiler in US English
A cooking device comprising a source of radiative and convective heat and a means of holding food above it; a barbecue.
definitions.example: I put some peppers and mushrooms on the grill to go with dinner.

definitions.definition: Food (designed to be) cooked on a grill.
definitions.example: a packet of frozen cauliflower cheese grills

definitions.definition: A grillroom; a restaurant serving grilled food.
definitions.example: These coupons will get you a discount at Johnny's Bar and Grill.

definitions.definition: A type of jewelry worn on the front teeth.
(by extension) The front teeth regarded collectively.
To cook (food) on a grill; to barbecue.
definitions.example: Why don't we get together Saturday and grill some burgers?

definitions.definition: To cook food under the element of a stove or only under the top element of an oven – broil, salamander.
To interrogate; to question aggressively or harshly.
definitions.example: The police grilled him about his movements at the time of the crime.

definitions.definition: To feel very hot; to swelter.
To stamp or mark with a grill.
A soldier in a small independent group, fighting against the government or regular forces by surprise raids.
A non-official war carried out by small independent groups; a guerrilla war.
Relating to, using, or typical of guerrilla warfare, or its principles of small independent or non-official perpetrators.
Relating to, using, or typical of guerrilla marketing.
A professional writer who is paid to write material that is officially credited to another person; one who writes on behalf of someone else, often for a celebrity.
definitions.example: Smith was listed as the author of his autobiography, but a ghostwriter did most of the work.

definitions.definition: (epigraphy) A misprint (or occasionally a scribal error) that affects a letter.
A value, as opposed to an identifier, written into the source code of a computer program.
A propositional variable or the negation of a propositional variable. Wp
Exactly as stated; read or understood without additional interpretation; according to the letter or verbal expression; real; not figurative or metaphorical.
definitions.example: The literal translation is “hands full of bananas” but it means empty-handed.

definitions.definition: Following the letter or exact words; not free; not taking liberties.
definitions.example: A literal reading of the law would prohibit it, but that is clearly not the intent.

definitions.definition: Consisting of, or expressed by, letters.
definitions.example: a literal equation

definitions.definition: (of a person) Giving a strict or literal construction; unimaginative; matter-of-fact.
Used non-literally as an intensifier; see literally for usage notes.
definitions.example: Telemarketers are the literal worst.

definitions.definition: An expression of grief, suffering, sadness or regret.
A song expressing grief.
To express grief; to weep or wail; to mourn.
To feel great sorrow or regret; to bewail.
Shine, polish or sparkle.
definitions.example: He polished the brass doorknob to a high luster.

definitions.definition: By extension, brilliance, attractiveness or splendor.
definitions.example: After so many years in the same field, the job had lost its luster.

definitions.definition: Refinement, polish or quality.
definitions.example: He spoke with all the lustre a seasoned enthusiast should have.

definitions.definition: A candlestick, chandelier, girandole, etc. generally of an ornamental character.
A substance that imparts lustre to a surface, such as plumbago or a glaze.
Lusterware.
A fabric of wool and cotton with a lustrous surface, used for women's dresses.
To gleam, have luster.
To give luster, distinguish.
To give a coating or other treatment to impart physical luster.
Effort expended on a particular task; toil, work.
That which requires hard work for its accomplishment; that which demands effort.
Workers in general; the working class, the workforce; sometimes specifically the labour movement, organised labour.
A political party or force aiming or claiming to represent the interests of labour.
The act of a mother giving birth.
The time period during which a mother gives birth.
The pitching or tossing of a vessel which results in the straining of timbers and rigging.
An old measure of land area in Mexico and Texas, approximately 177 acres.
To toil, to work.
To belabour, to emphasise or expand upon (a point in a debate, etc).
definitions.example: I think we've all got the idea. There's no need to labour the point.

definitions.definition: To be oppressed with difficulties or disease; to do one's work under conditions which make it especially hard or wearisome; to move slowly, as against opposition, or under a burden.
To suffer the pangs of childbirth.
To pitch or roll heavily, as a ship in a turbulent sea.
An early stage of growth for some insects and amphibians, in which after hatching from their egg, insects are wingless and resemble a caterpillar or grub, and amphibians lack limbs and resemble fish.
An animal in the aforementioned stage.
A form of a recently born or hatched animal that is quite different from its adult stage.
A large house or building, usually built for the wealthy.
A luxurious flat (apartment).
A house provided for a clergyman; a manse.
A stopping-place during a journey; a stage.
An astrological house; a station of the moon.
One of twenty-eight sections of the sky.
(chiefly in the plural) An individual habitation or apartment within a large house or group of buildings. (Now chiefly in allusion to John 14:2.)
Any of the branches of the Rastafari movement.
The middle of the night: the sixth temporal hour, equidistant between sunset and sunrise.
12 o'clock at night exactly.
Utterly dark or black.
A legally or socially binding conceptual contract of entitlement to wealth, void of intrinsic value, payable for all debts and taxes, and regulated in supply.
A generally accepted means of exchange and measure of value.
definitions.example: Before colonial times cowry shells imported from Mauritius were used as money in Western Africa.

definitions.definition: A currency maintained by a state or other entity which can guarantee its value (such as a monetary union).
definitions.example: money supply;  money market

definitions.definition: Hard cash in the form of banknotes and coins, as opposed to cheques/checks, credit cards, or credit more generally.
The total value of liquid assets available for an individual or other economic unit, such as cash and bank deposits.
Wealth; a person, family or class that possesses wealth
definitions.example: He married money.

definitions.definition: An item of value between two or more parties used for the exchange of goods or services.
A person who funds an operation.
One of the triune goddesses of the Lady in Wicca alongside the Crone and Mother representing a girl or a young woman
A girl or an unmarried young woman.
A female virgin.
definitions.example: She's unmarried and still a maiden.

definitions.definition: A man with no experience of sex, especially because of deliberate abstention.
A maidservant.
A clothes maiden.
An unmarried woman, especially an older woman.
A racehorse without any victory, i.e. one having a "virgin record".
A horse race in which all starters are maidens.
A Scottish counterpart of the guillotine.
A maiden over.
A machine for washing linen.
Virgin.
(of a female, human or animal) Without offspring.
Like or befitting a (young, unmarried) maiden.
Being a first occurrence or event.
definitions.example: After Edmund Burke's maiden speech, William Pitt the Elder said Burke had "spoken in such a manner as to stop the mouths of all Europe" and that the Commons should congratulate itself on acquiring such a member.

definitions.definition: Being an over in which no runs are scored.
Fresh; innocent; unpolluted; pure; hitherto unused.
(of a fortress) Never having been captured or violated.
(of a tree) Grown from seed and never pruned
Matter which may be shaped or manipulated, particularly in making something.
definitions.example: Asphalt, composed of oil and sand, is a widely used material for roads.

definitions.definition: Text written for a specific purpose.
definitions.example: We were a warm-up act at the time; we didn't have enough original material to headline.

definitions.definition: A sample or specimens for study.
Cloth to be made into a garment. Fabric.
definitions.example: You'll need about a yard of material to make this.

definitions.definition: The people collectively who are qualified for a certain position or activity.
definitions.example: He is not the only one. I believe we have lots of presidential material in various public offices.

definitions.definition: Related data of various kinds, especially if collected as the basis for a document or book.
The substance that something is made or composed of.
All of a player's pieces and pawns on the chessboard.
To form from matter; to materialize.
Having to do with matter; consisting of matter.
definitions.example: This compound has a number of interesting material properties.

definitions.definition: Worldly, as opposed to spiritual.
definitions.example: Don't let material concerns get in the way of living a happy life.

definitions.definition: Significant.
definitions.example: This is the most material fact in this lawsuit.

definitions.definition: An alcoholic drink similar to apple brandy and calvados, made by freeze-distilling ("jacking") apple cider.
A small recessed area set off from a larger room.
A shady retreat.
definitions.example: The house was situated in a leafy alcove.

definitions.definition: A female who performs on the stage or in films.
A female doer or "actor" (in a general sense).
Something that surrounds; encompassing material, substance or shape.
The atmosphere; the surrounding air or sky; atmospheric components collectively such as air, clouds, water vapour, hail, etc.
A type of modern music that creates a relaxing and peaceful atmosphere.
Encompassing on all sides; surrounding; encircling; enveloping.
definitions.example: A cup of tea eventually cools to the ambient temperature.

definitions.definition: Evoking or creating an atmosphere: atmospheric.
Relating to, or suitable for, storage at room temperature.
definitions.example: ambient food

definitions.definition: Containing objects or describing a setting that one is interested in.
A person who is very old.
A person who lived in ancient times.
One of the senior members of the Inns of Court or of Chancery.
A senior; an elder; a predecessor.
Having lasted from a remote period; having been of long duration; of great age, very old.
definitions.example: an ancient city  an ancient forest

definitions.definition: Existent or occurring in time long past, usually in remote ages; belonging to or associated with antiquity; old, as opposed to modern.
definitions.example: an ancient author  an ancient empire

definitions.definition: (history) Relating to antiquity as a primarily European historical period; the time before the Middle Ages.
Experienced; versed.
Former; sometime.
To spoil or ruin (one's chance of success)
To violently provoke (a child) in the belief that this will make them more assertive.
Intentionally causing or reveling in pain and suffering; merciless, heartless.
definitions.example: The supervisor was very cruel to Josh, as he would always give Josh the hardest, most degrading work he could find.

definitions.definition: Harsh; severe.
Cool; awesome; neat.
To a great degree; terribly.
A strong fortress that sits high above a city.
(sometimes figurative) A stronghold or fortified place.
An armoured portion of a warship, housing important equipment.
A Salvation Army meeting place.
A device in which a mixture of denser and lighter materials (normally dispersed in a liquid) is separated by being spun about a central axis at high speed.
An apparatus in which humans are spun to simulate acceleration in an aircraft or spacecraft.
To rotate something in a centrifuge in order to separate its constituents
A complete assembly, consisting of an artillery tube and a breech mechanism, firing mechanism or base cap, which is a component of a gun, howitzer or mortar. It may include muzzle appendages.
Any similar device for shooting material out of a tube.
A bone of a horse's leg, between the fetlock joint and the knee or hock.
A cannon bit.
A large muzzle-loading artillery piece.
A carom.
definitions.example: In English billiards, a cannon is when one's cue ball strikes the other player's cue ball and the red ball on the same shot; and it is worth two points.

definitions.definition: The arm of a player that can throw well.
definitions.example: He's got a cannon out in right.

definitions.definition: A hollow cylindrical piece carried by a revolving shaft, on which it may, however, revolve independently.
(Chinese chess) A piece which moves horizontally and vertically like a rook but captures another piece by jumping over a different piece in the line of attack.
To bombard with cannons.
To play the carom billiard shot. To strike two balls with the cue ball
definitions.example: The white cannoned off the red onto the pink.

definitions.definition: To fire something, especially spherical, rapidly.
To collide or strike violently, especially so as to glance off or rebound.
A generally accepted principle; a rule.
definitions.example: The trial must proceed according to the canons of law.

definitions.definition: A group of literary works that are generally accepted as representing a field.
The works of a writer that have been accepted as authentic.
definitions.example: the entire Shakespeare canon

definitions.definition: A eucharistic prayer, particularly the Roman Canon.
A religious law or body of law decreed by the church.
definitions.example: We must proceed according to canon law.

definitions.definition: A catalogue of saints acknowledged and canonized in the Roman Catholic Church.
In monasteries, a book containing the rules of a religious order.
A member of a cathedral chapter; one who possesses a prebend in a cathedral or collegiate church.
A piece of music in which the same melody is played by different voices, but beginning at different times; a round.
definitions.example: Pachelbel’s Canon has become very popular.

definitions.definition: (Roman law) A rent or stipend payable at some regular time, generally annual, e.g., canon frumentarius
Those sources, especially including literary works, which are considered part of the main continuity regarding a given fictional universe.
definitions.example: A spin-off book series revealed the aliens to be originally from Earth, but it's not canon.

definitions.definition: A rolled and filleted loin of meat; also called cannon.
definitions.example: a canon of beef or lamb

definitions.definition: A large size of type formerly used for printing the church canons, standardized as 48-point.
The part of a bell by which it is suspended; the ear or shank of a bell.
A carom.
A clergy member serving a cathedral or collegiate church.
A canon regular, a member of any of several Roman Catholic religious orders.
A Near Eastern and Caucasian musical instrument related to the zither, dulcimer, or harp having either 26 strings and a single bridge, or twice that number and two bridges.
One who cheats.
An improvised breaker bar made from a length of pipe and a wrench (spanner), usually used to free screws, bolts, etc. that are difficult to remove with a ratchet or wrench alone.

A closed curve, the locus of a point such that the sum of the distances from that point to two other fixed points (called the foci of the ellipse) is constant; equivalently, the conic section that is the intersection of a cone with a plane that does not intersect the base of the cone.
(grammar) To remove from a phrase a word which is grammatically needed, but which is clearly understood without having to be stated.
To fill with rage; to provoke to frenzy or madness; to make furious.
Angered, made furious, made full of rage.
Insane, mad.
Having no end.
definitions.example: endless time; endless praise

definitions.definition: Extending indefinitely.
definitions.example: an endless line

definitions.definition: Without profitable end; fruitless; unsatisfying.
Any of several large carnivorous and carrion-eating birds in the family Accipitridae, having a powerful hooked bill and keen vision.
A gold coin with a face value of ten dollars, formerly used in the United States.
A 13th-century coin minted in Europe and circulated in England as a debased sterling silver penny, outlawed under Edward I.
A score of two under par for a hole.
To score an eagle.
To raise (something) to a higher position.
definitions.example: The doctor told me elevating my legs would help reduce the swelling.

definitions.definition: To promote (someone) to a higher rank.
To confer honor or nobility on (someone).
definitions.example: The traditional worldview elevates man as the pinnacle of creation.

definitions.definition: To make (something or someone) more worthy or of greater value.
definitions.example: A talented chef can elevate everyday ingredients into gourmet delights.

definitions.definition: To direct (the mind, thoughts, etc.) toward more worthy things.
To increase the intensity or degree of (something).
definitions.example: Some drugs have the side effect of elevating your blood sugar level.

definitions.definition: To lift the spirits of (someone)
To intoxicate in a slight degree; to make (someone) tipsy.
(Latinism) To attempt to make (something) seem less important, remarkable, etc.
An elevated railway.
Raised, particularly above ground level.
Increased, particularly above a normal level.
definitions.example: The patient presented with elevated blood pressure.

definitions.definition: Of a higher rank or status.
Running with administration rights granted
definitions.example: Install all the required tools from an elevated console.

definitions.definition: Intoxicated; drunk

A substance (of molecular formula H₂O) found at room temperature and pressure as a clear liquid; it is present naturally as rain, and found in rivers, lakes and seas; its solid form is ice and its gaseous form is steam.
definitions.example: By the action of electricity, the water was resolved into its two parts, oxygen and hydrogen.

definitions.definition: The aforementioned liquid, considered one of the Classical elements or basic elements of alchemy.
definitions.example: And God said, Let the waters under the heaven be gathered together unto one place, and let the dry land appear: and it was so.

definitions.definition: Water in a body; an area of open water.
definitions.example: The boat was found within the territorial waters.

definitions.definition: A body of water, almost always a river.
A combination of water and other substance(s).
(in the plural or in the singular) A state of affairs; conditions; usually with an adjective indicating an adverse condition.
definitions.example: The rough waters of change will bring about the calm after the storm.

definitions.definition: A person's intuition.
definitions.example: I know he'll succeed. I feel it in my waters.

definitions.definition: Excess valuation of securities.
The limpidity and lustre of a precious stone, especially a diamond.
definitions.example: a diamond of the first water is perfectly pure and transparent

definitions.definition: A wavy, lustrous pattern or decoration such as is imparted to linen, silk, metals, etc.
The gray wolf, specifically all subspecies of the gray wolf (Canis lupus) that are not dingoes or dogs.
definitions.example: Check out my pet. He's a wolf.

definitions.definition: A man who makes amorous advances to many women.
A wolf tone or wolf note.
definitions.example: The soft violin solo was marred by persistent wolves.

definitions.definition: Any very ravenous, rapacious, or destructive person or thing; especially, want; starvation.
definitions.example: They toiled hard to keep the wolf from the door.

definitions.definition: One of the destructive, and usually hairy, larvae of several species of beetles and grain moths.
A white worm, or maggot, which infests granaries.
An eating ulcer or sore. See lupus.
A willying machine.
To devour; to gobble; to eat (something) voraciously.
To make amorous advances to many women; to hit on women; to cruise for sex.
To hunt for wolves.
An instrument of attack or defense in combat or hunting, e.g. most guns, missiles, or swords.
definitions.example: The club that is now mostly used for golf was once a common weapon.

definitions.definition: An instrument or other means of harming or exerting control over another.
definitions.example: Money is the main weapon of modern oligarchs.

definitions.definition: A tool of any kind.
definitions.example: Choose your weapon.

definitions.definition: An idiot, an oaf, a fool, a tool; a contemptible or incompetent person.
Soaked with water
In danger of sinking because of excess water onboard
Outside, externally. This is still used in the names of some civil parishes in England, e.g. St Cuthbert Without.
Lacking something.
definitions.example: Being from a large, poor family, he learned to live without.

definitions.definition: In prostitution: without a condom being worn.
Outside of, beyond.
definitions.example: The snow was swirling without the cottage, but it was warm within.

definitions.definition: Not having, containing, characteristic of, etc.
definitions.example: It was a mistake to leave my house without a coat.

definitions.definition: Not doing or not having done something.
definitions.example: He likes to eat everything without sharing.

definitions.definition: Unless, except (introducing a clause).
The point where two components of a structure join, but are still able to rotate.
definitions.example: This rod is free to swing at the joint with the platform.

definitions.definition: The point where two components of a structure join rigidly.
definitions.example: The water is leaking out of the joint between the two pipes.

definitions.definition: Any part of the body where two bones join, in most cases allowing that part of the body to be bent or straightened.
The means of securing together the meeting surfaces of components of a structure.
definitions.example: The dovetail joint, while more difficult to make, is also quite strong.

definitions.definition: A cut of meat.
definitions.example: Set the joint in a roasting tin and roast for the calculated cooking time.

definitions.definition: The part or space included between two joints, knots, nodes, or articulations.
definitions.example: a joint of cane or of a grass stem; a joint of the leg

definitions.definition: A fracture in which the strata are not offset; a geologic joint.
(somewhat derogatory) A place of business, particularly in the food service or hospitality industries.
definitions.example: It was the kind of joint you wouldn't want your boss to see you in.

definitions.definition: (with the definite article) Prison.
definitions.example: I'm just trying to stay out of the joint.

definitions.definition: A marijuana cigarette.
definitions.example: After locking the door and closing the shades, they lit the joint.

definitions.definition: A syringe used to inject an illicit drug.
The penis.
To unite by a joint or joints; to fit together; to prepare so as to fit together
definitions.example: a jointing plane

definitions.definition: To join; to connect; to unite; to combine.
To provide with a joint or joints; to articulate.
To separate the joints; of; to divide at the joint or joints; to disjoint; to cut up into joints, as meat.
To fit as if by joints; to coalesce as joints do.
definitions.example: the stones joint, neatly.

definitions.definition: Done by two or more people or organisations working together.
definitions.example: The play was a joint production between the two companies.

definitions.definition: An almost transparent aquatic animal; any one of the acalephs, especially one of the larger species, having a jellylike appearance.
(Jewish history) A special year of emancipation supposed to be kept every fifty years, when farming was abandoned and Hebrew slaves were set free.
A 25th, 40th, 50th, 60th or 70th anniversary.
A special year (originally held every hundred years, then fifty, and then fewer) in which remission from sin could be granted as well as indulgences upon making a pilgrimage to Rome.
A time of celebration or rejoicing.
An occasion of mass manumission from slavery.
A period of fifty years; a half-century.
To cast a spell on.
To bring bad luck to.
To cause something to happen by mentioning it, usually sarcastically.
To bump into or brush against while in motion; to push aside.
To move through by pushing and shoving.
To be close to or in physical contact with.
To contend or vie in order to acquire something.
To pick or attempt to pick pockets.
An act of jostling; a push or shove.
A distinct physical object.
definitions.example: Tweezers are great for manipulating small items.

definitions.definition: (by extension) An object that can be picked up for later use.
A line of text having a legal or other meaning; a separate particular in an account.
definitions.example: In response to the first item, we deny all wrongdoing.

definitions.definition: (psychometrics) A question on a test, which may include its answers.
definitions.example: The exam has 100 items, each of which includes a correct response and three distractors.

definitions.definition: A matter for discussion in an agenda.
definitions.example: The first item for discussion is the budget for next year's picnic.

definitions.definition: Two people who are having a relationship with each other.
definitions.example: Jack and Jill are an item.

definitions.definition: A short article in a newspaper.
definitions.example: an item concerning the weather

definitions.definition: A hint; an innuendo.
To make a note of.
Between the stars.
definitions.example: It can take centuries for light to travel interstellar distances.

definitions.definition: Among the stars.
definitions.example: Space is littered with interstellar particles.

definitions.definition: (operations) The stock of an item on hand at a particular location or business.
definitions.example: Due to an undersized inventory at the Boston outlet, customers had to travel to Providence to find the item.

definitions.definition: (operations) A detailed list of all of the items on hand.
definitions.example: The inventory included several items that one wouldn't normally think to find at a cheese shop.

definitions.definition: (operations) The process of producing or updating such a list.
definitions.example: This month's inventory took nearly three days.

definitions.definition: A space containing the items available to a character for immediate use.
definitions.example: You can't get through the underground tunnel if there are more than three items in your inventory.

definitions.definition: (operations) To take stock of the resources or items on hand; to produce an inventory.
definitions.example: The main job of the night shift was to inventory the store, and restock when necessary.

definitions.definition: Something that is infinite in nature.
Indefinably large, countlessly great; immense.
Boundless, endless, without end or limits; innumerable.
(with plural noun) Infinitely many.
Greater than any positive quantity or magnitude; limitless.
(of a set) Having infinitely many elements.
(grammar) Not limited by person or number.
Capable of endless repetition; said of certain forms of the canon, also called perpetual fugues, constructed so that their ends lead to their beginnings.
Infinitely many.
An illiterate person, one not able to read and write.
A person ignorant about a given subject.
definitions.example: The government is run by business illiterates.

definitions.definition: Unable to read and write.
Having less than an expected standard of familiarity with language and literature, or having little formal education.
Not conforming to prescribed standards of speech or writing.
Ignorant in a specified way or about a specified subject.
definitions.example: economically illiterate, emotionally illiterate

definitions.definition: To arrive, come (to a place).
To come about; happen; come into being; arise.
definitions.example: It hath becomen so that many a man had to sterve.

definitions.definition: Begin to be; turn into.
definitions.example: She became a doctor when she was 25.

definitions.definition: To be proper for; to beseem.
Of an adornment, piece of clothing etc.: to look attractive on (someone).
definitions.example: That dress really becomes you.

definitions.definition: A container, typically made of glass or plastic and having a tapered neck, used primarily for holding liquids.
definitions.example: Beer is often sold in bottles.

definitions.definition: The contents of such a container.
definitions.example: I only drank a bottle of beer.

definitions.definition: A container with a rubber nipple used for giving liquids to infants, a baby bottle.
definitions.example: The baby wants a bottle.

definitions.definition: Nerve, courage.
definitions.example: He was going to ask her out, but he lost his bottle when he saw her.

definitions.definition: (of a person with a particular hair color) A container of hair dye, hence with one’s hair color produced by dyeing.
definitions.example: Did you know he’s a bottle brunette? His natural hair color is strawberry blonde.

definitions.definition: A bundle, especially of hay; something tied in a bundle.
Intoxicating liquor; alcohol.
definitions.example: to drown one’s troubles in the bottle

definitions.definition: The tendency of pages printed several on a sheet to rotate slightly when the sheet is folded two or more times.
To seal (a liquid) into a bottle for later consumption. Also fig.
definitions.example: This plant bottles vast quantities of spring water every day.

definitions.definition: To feed (an infant) baby formula.
definitions.example: Because of complications she can't breast feed her baby and so she bottles him.

definitions.definition: To refrain from doing (something) at the last moment because of a sudden loss of courage.
definitions.example: The rider bottled the big jump.

definitions.definition: To throw away a leading position.
definitions.example: Liverpool bottled the Premier League.

definitions.definition: To strike (someone) with a bottle.
definitions.example: He was bottled at a nightclub and had to have facial surgery.

definitions.definition: To pelt (a musical act on stage, etc.) with bottles as a sign of disapproval.
definitions.example: Meat Loaf was once bottled at Reading Festival.

definitions.definition: To perform (a task) in an unacceptable or incompetent manner; to make a mess of something
definitions.example: A botched haircut seems to take forever to grow out.

definitions.definition: To do something without skill, without care, or clumsily.
To repair or mend clumsily.
Made or repaired in a clumsy or incompetent manner.
The state behind or past.
(of motion) In the direction towards the back.
definitions.example: They left without a backward glance.

definitions.definition: (of motion) In the direction reverse of normal.
definitions.example: The occasional backward movement of planets is evidence they revolve around the sun.

definitions.definition: Reluctant or unable to advance.
Of a culture: undeveloped or unsophisticated.
Outdated.
On that part of the field behind the batsman's popping crease.
Further behind the batsman's popping crease than something else.
Unwilling; averse; reluctant.
Slow to apprehend; having difficulties in learning.
definitions.example: a backward child

definitions.definition: Late or behindhand.
definitions.example: a backward season

definitions.definition: Already past or gone; bygone.
(of motion) In the direction towards the back; backwards
definitions.example: to throw the arms backward

definitions.definition: Toward, or in, past time or events; ago.
By way of reflection; reflexively.
From a better to a worse state, as from honor to shame, from religion to sin.
An item (usually rope, tape, or string) used to hold two or more things together.
The spine of a book where the pages are held together.
A finishing on a seam or hem of a garment.
The association of a named item with an element of a program.
The interface of a library with a programming language other than one it is written in.
definitions.example: The Python binding is automatically generated.

definitions.definition: The action or result of making two or more molecules stick together.
A member of the Macropodidae family of large marsupials with strong hind legs for hopping, native to Australia.
A hooded jacket with a front pocket, usually of fleece material, a kangaroo jacket.
To practice kangaroo care on an infant; to hold a premature infant against the skin.
To hunt kangaroo.
To move like a kangaroo
Of or relating to Australia.
(investments) Characterized by rapidly jumping prices in securities markets or by belief that the prices are unstable in contrast to bear and bull markets.
The act of killing.
definitions.example: The assassin liked to make a clean kill, and thus favored small arms over explosives.

definitions.definition: Specifically, the death blow.
definitions.example: The hunter delivered the kill with a pistol shot to the head.

definitions.definition: The result of killing; that which has been killed.
definitions.example: The fox dragged its kill back to its den.

definitions.definition: The grounding of the ball on the opponent's court, winning the rally.
To put to death; to extinguish the life of.
definitions.example: Smoking kills more people each year than alcohol and drugs combined.

definitions.definition: To render inoperative.
definitions.example: He killed the engine and turned off the headlights, but remained in the car, waiting.

definitions.definition: To stop, cease or render void; to terminate.
definitions.example: My computer wouldn't respond until I killed some of the running processes.

definitions.definition: To amaze, exceed, stun or otherwise incapacitate.
definitions.example: That joke always kills me.

definitions.definition: To cause great pain, discomfort or distress to.
definitions.example: These tight shoes are killing my feet.

definitions.definition: To produce feelings of dissatisfaction or revulsion in.
definitions.example: It kills me to learn how many poor people are practically starving in this country while rich moguls spend such outrageous amounts on useless luxuries.

definitions.definition: To use up or to waste.
definitions.example: He told the bartender, pointing at the bottle of scotch he planned to consume, "Leave it, I'm going to kill the bottle."

definitions.definition: To exert an overwhelming effect on.
definitions.example: Between the two of us, we killed the rest of the case of beer.

definitions.definition: To overpower, overwhelm or defeat.
definitions.example: The team had absolutely killed their traditional rivals, and the local sports bars were raucous with celebrations.

definitions.definition: To force a company out of business.
To produce intense pain.
definitions.example: You don't ever want to get rabies. The doctor will have to give you multiple shots and they really kill.

definitions.definition: To punish severely.
definitions.example: My parents are going to kill me!

definitions.definition: To strike (a ball, etc.) with such force and placement as to make a shot that is impossible to defend against, usually winning a point.
To cause (a ball, etc.) to be out of play, resulting in a stoppage of gameplay.
To succeed with an audience, especially in comedy.
To cause to assume the value zero.
(IRC) To disconnect (a user) involuntarily from the network.
To deadmelt.
(north-east US) A creek; a body of water; a channel or arm of the sea.
definitions.example: Schuylkill, Catskill, etc.

definitions.definition: A kiln.
(etc.) A set of keys used to operate a typewriter, computer etc.
A component of many instruments including the piano, organ, and harpsichord consisting of usually black and white keys that cause different tones to be produced when struck.
A device with keys of a musical keyboard, used to control electronic sound-producing devices which may be built into or separate from the keyboard device.
To type on a computer keyboard.
definitions.example: Keyboarding is the part of this job I hate the most.

definitions.definition: A kitten or young cat.
(sometimes capitalized) A pet name for a cat.
definitions.example: Aw, look at the kitty!

definitions.definition: A money pool, as for a card game, or for shared expenses.
In a home game, a small, specified amount taken from each pot to pay the host's expenses.
Female genitals. (Euphemistic form of pussy.)
A set of additional cards dealt face down in some games.
A circular wooden vessel, made of hooped staves.
A kind of basket made especially from straw of rushes, especially for holding fish; by extension, the contents of such a basket or similar container, used as a measure of weight.
definitions.example: 1961 18 Jan, Guardian (cited after OED):

definitions.definition: A collection of items forming the equipment of a soldier, carried in a knapsack.
Any collection of items needed for a specific purpose, especially for use by a workman, or personal effects packed for travelling.
definitions.example: Always carry a good first-aid kit.

definitions.definition: A collection of parts sold for the buyer to assemble.
definitions.example: I built the entire car from a kit.

definitions.definition: The set of skills and abilities chosen for a playable character.
The standard set of clothing, accessories and equipment worn by players.
Clothing.
definitions.example: Get your kit off and come to bed.

definitions.definition: A full software distribution, as opposed to a patch or upgrade.
A drum kit.
To assemble or collect something into kits or sets or to give somebody a kit. See also kit out and other derived phrases.
definitions.example: We need to kit the parts for the assembly by Friday, so that manufacturing can build the tool.

definitions.definition: A kitten (young cat).
A kit fox.
A young skunk.
A school of pigeons, especially domesticated, trained pigeons.

A very small long-necked violin that came in a variety of shapes and configurations, meant to be carried in a pocket and intended for instructors (dancing masters) to carry to accompany their students.
